metadata.unit_note: Dashboard display units use SI or SI-derived units where applicable: km2 for area, km for length, m/mm for depths and rainfall, and 2026 USD for money.
metadata.currency_note: Monetary values are converted from source INR lakh to 2026 USD using 1 USD = INR 96.0622.
metadata.impact_rollup_note: District/state impact totals apportion event-level reported impacts equally across the districts touched by each event to avoid multiplying national impacts after district explosion.
metadata.sanity_cap_note: Values marked by source sanity-cap flags are excluded from dashboard rollups.

papers.title: India flood inventory: creation of a multi-source national geospatial database to facilitate comprehensive flood research
A district-level flood severity index for flood management in India

methodology.method: The dashboard reads the v7 India Flood Inventory workbook and uses the event-district exploded records that contain a valid GADM district identifier.
methodology.interpretation: Each row represents one event linked to one district, so multi-district events can appear once per affected district.

methodology.method: Records are filtered with the source GADM match-valid flag when present, then duplicate district-event pairs are removed using GID_2 and the event identifier.
methodology.interpretation: District, state, and cause summaries are based on unique event-district records rather than repeated source rows.

methodology.method: Frequency is the count of unique IFI events mapped to each district after the validity and de-duplication steps.
methodology.interpretation: This is a district exposure-to-recorded-events measure, not a count of newspaper articles or raw database rows.

methodology.method: The source-provided IFI flood hazard score is read from the v7 hazard-score field. The dashboard reports the district 95th percentile of scored event records, and only classifies hazard where at least three scored events are available.
methodology.interpretation: P95 hazard is a high-end severity indicator on a 0-100 scale; districts with too few scored events are treated as insufficient evidence for hazard-class mapping.

methodology.method: Districts with events and sufficient scored records are split into low, medium, and high tertiles for event frequency and P95 hazard, then combined into a 3 by 3 frequency-severity class.
methodology.interpretation: The hotspot layer highlights whether a district is high because floods are frequent, severe, or both.

methodology.method: Priority is 100 x (0.42 x event-frequency percentile + 0.38 x P95 hazard/100 + 0.20 x fatality percentile). Fatalities use apportioned district totals, and districts without mapped events receive zero.
methodology.interpretation: The score ranks districts for screening and planning attention; it is a composite decision-support index, not a probability forecast.

methodology.method: Cause groups are taken from the classified cause field in the inventory and harmonized into the dashboard labels used in maps, charts, and tables.
methodology.interpretation: Cause charts describe classified event-district records, so the same event may contribute to multiple districts if it affected a broader area.

methodology.method: Monthly tables group event-district records by start month and cause. Values are row-normalized percentages within each cause, and the row label shows the cause total as n.
methodology.interpretation: Each row answers: within this cause group, what share of records begin in each month?

methodology.method: Event-level fatalities, displaced people, crop area, and losses are apportioned equally across affected districts before district and state totals are calculated.
methodology.interpretation: Apportionment avoids multiplying national impacts when one event touches several districts.

methodology.method: Area is shown as square kilometres, crop area is converted from hectares to km2, and monetary loss is converted from INR lakh to 2026 USD using 1 USD = INR 96.0622.
methodology.interpretation: Displayed values use SI or SI-derived units and a consistent 2026 USD monetary basis.

methodology.method: Reported impact values marked by source sanity-cap flags are excluded from dashboard rollups before apportionment.
methodology.interpretation: This keeps extreme or flagged source values from dominating decision-support summaries.

methodology.method: The export button writes the currently filtered district-level metrics, including the selected map score, impact fields, and ranking indicators.
methodology.interpretation: Exports are intended for quick screening and follow-up analysis outside the dashboard.

metrics.label: Event frequency
metrics.description: Unique IFI events linked to each district.

metrics.label: P95 hazard
metrics.description: District 95th percentile of the IFI hazard score.

metrics.label: Dominant cause
metrics.description: Most common classified cause among district-linked events.

metrics.label: Fatalities
metrics.description: Event-level reported fatalities apportioned across affected districts.

metrics.label: Crop area
metrics.description: Reported crop area affected, converted from hectares to square kilometres and apportioned across affected districts.

metrics.label: Loss
metrics.description: Reported monetary loss converted from INR lakh to 2026 USD and apportioned across affected districts.

metrics.label: Planning priority
metrics.description: Composite of event frequency, P95 hazard, and apportioned fatalities.

monthly.note: Rows show monthly share within each cause group. Small cause totals can produce large percentages from few records.
